original_hypothesis.title: AccessControlHooks addRoleProvider() external call to provider.isPullProvider() can be abused to revert and block provider onboarding (admin DoS)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: AccessControlHooks.addRoleProvider makes an external call to providerAddress.isPullProvider() without handling reverts. A reverting/malicious providerAddress prevents onboarding (or updating) that provider entry.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Same as FixedTermLoanHooks; operational risk if provider contracts are untrusted or can change behavior.
deployment_assumptions: Borrower attempts to add a providerAddress that is buggy/malicious or later changes behavior
Provider onboarding is required for continued market operation

proof_of_signal.pattern: unguarded external call in admin path

preconditions: providerAddress reverts or gas-griefs on isPullProvider()

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: admin cannot add provider; operational DoS

analysis_questions: Is try/catch desirable or should this revert loudly by design?
Could metamorphic contracts change isPullProvider behavior post-approval and disrupt expected flows?
validation_requirements: Simulate revert in isPullProvider and confirm addRoleProvider fails
Decide on hardening approach if desired

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Borrower mistakenly selects a provider address that always reverts; provider onboarding is blocked and access control cannot be updated accordingly.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Borrower can choose a different provider

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing negative tests for isPullProvider revert

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
